page: Microsoft Azure NG16ads_V620_v1 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the NGADSV620v1 family, virtualized via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor and is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This server configuration includes a fractional GPU allocation of 0.5 AMD V620 GPU, providing 15 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is bundled, offering 2444 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is premium storage capable. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included in this profile.

The hardware profile presents a specific tradeoff between compute resources and memory capacity, featuring a relatively low RAM-to-vCPU ratio of 1:2. The inclusion of a fractional GPU and substantial local storage makes this instance a specialized option for workloads requiring hardware-accelerated graphics or parallel processing alongside high-capacity local storage, without the cost of a full dedicated GPU.

Appropriate workloads for this configuration include cloud gaming, virtual desktop infrastructure, hardware-accelerated video encoding, and database applications that benefit from local storage and moderate GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure NG16ads_V620_v1 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the NGADSV620v1 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The hardware configuration includes a fractional GPU allocation of 0.5 V620 GPU with 15 GB of VRAM, alongside 2444 GB of local storage. This server is premium storage capable but does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The resource profile represents a qualitative balance for workloads requiring moderate GPU acceleration and high local storage capacity without the expense of a full GPU. It is suited for cloud gaming, virtual desktop hosting, video transcoding, and database workloads that leverage local disk storage and hardware-accelerated rendering.
